title: monitoring — trait composition
description: Five owned traits. `BelongsToHealthCheck` is the FK trait for downstream HealthCheckRun. `HasHealthCheckRuns` exposes the inverse scoped queries. `BelongsToMonitoringAlertPolicy` is the FK trait for MonitoringAlert. `BelongsToMonitoringIncident` is the FK trait (nullable) on MonitoringAlert. `HasMonitoringAlerts` exposes the inverse on MonitoringAlertPolicy + MonitoringIncident. Order matters when composed with BelongsToTenant — tenant MUST come first so the scope chain observes the tenant context.

owned.description: Mixed into HealthCheckRun. Adds the `healthCheck()` belongsTo relation. Refuses to save a row whose health_check_id points at a check in a different tenant than the row itself (defense-in-depth against cross-tenant FK smuggling).
composition_notes: Order: `BelongsToTenant` must appear BEFORE `BelongsToHealthCheck` in the trait list.

owned.description: Composed onto the HealthCheck model. Adds `->runs()` (hasMany over HealthCheckRun), `->recentRuns($seconds)` (scope for the trailing window), `->latestRun()` (most recent run for status queries), `->recentFailureCount($seconds)` (helper for the AlertPolicyEvaluator).
Mixed into MonitoringAlert. Adds the `policy()` belongsTo relation. Refuses cross-tenant FK.
Mixed into MonitoringAlert. Adds the `incident()` belongsTo relation. NULLABLE — a firing alert may not yet be grouped into an incident (GroupAlertsIntoIncidentJob attaches it asynchronously).

Composed onto the MonitoringAlertPolicy AND MonitoringIncident models. Adds `->alerts()` (hasMany), `->firingAlerts()` (scope status='firing'), `->acknowledgedAlerts()` (scope status='acknowledged'), `->resolvedAlerts()` (scope status='resolved'), `->hasFiringAlerts()` (bool helper used by the observer's soft-delete refusal).

framework.reason: HealthCheck, MonitoringAlertPolicy, MonitoringIncident, MonitoringProviderConfig. NOT HealthCheckRun (append-only). NOT MonitoringAlert (retained per the retention pipeline; hard-purged only).

foundation.reason: HealthCheck + MonitoringAlertPolicy + MonitoringIncident (searchable by name / slug / title). NOT HealthCheckRun + MonitoringAlert (accessed via relations from their parents).

tenants.reason: Every owned row carries tenant_id per tenancy-columns.md §3.

audit.reason: Every write to the six tables is compliance-relevant.

activity.reason: MonitoringAlertPolicy + MonitoringIncident + MonitoringProviderConfig — admin actions feed the staff-visible activity feed. NOT HealthCheck / HealthCheckRun / MonitoringAlert — either high-volume OR already covered by audit.

health_check_run.notes: NO SoftDeletes — runs are append-only; hard-purged by PurgeExpiredHealthCheckRunsJob only. NO HasActivityLog — high-volume.

monitoring_alert.notes: NO SoftDeletes — alerts are per-fire records; hard-purged only. NO HasActivityLog — high-volume.

monitoring_provider_config.notes: SoftDeletes for the deactivate path (soft-delete + 90-day grace before hard-purge). Auditable is critical — credentials + circuit-breaker state changes are compliance-relevant.
